practical facts. Havelock Ellis has all his planets between the
tenth and the fourth, with Saturn in the seventh very near
the hemisphere line. Thus there is a world of contrast between
these two. Human biological differences are brought by him
into a psychological perspective. Because of Saturn he was
sensitive to the deeper significances of experience. The posi
tion of Saturn determined his life work with the mal-adjust
ments and sickness; and yet Saturn has given him sensitive-

ness, depth ofthought and feeling and complete withdrawal

Our fourth chart is that of Marie Antoinette, with planets
between the fourth and the eighth. Isolated Saturn and iso
lated Mars are very striking here. Further Mercury is alone in
the sixth.
